summary: American novelist and academic, best known for Stoner (1965), a novel about a University of Missouri English professor. Also wrote Butcher's Crossing (a frontier novel) and Augustus (an epistolary novel about the Roman emperor). Note: shares his name with the famous film composer and an Australian classical guitarist, who appear in many of the same clue sets but are different people.

works.name: Stoner
works.description: Williams's most famous novel (1965) about William Stoner, a University of Missouri English professor who is inspired to study literature by Shakespeare's Sonnet 73. The protagonist has an affair with Kathrine Driscoll and feuds with the crippled colleague Hollis Lomax (aka Willy Lomax) for 20 years after savagely interrogating a blowhard student during a thesis defense. A 2018 Charles Shields biography called Williams 'The man who wrote the perfect novel.' The novel saw a dramatic rise in popularity in 2013 after being reissued by NYRB Classics, and Anna Gavalda's 2011 French translation helped make it a surprise bestseller in Europe.

clues.clue: About a University of Missouri English professor; called 'the perfect novel' by biographer Charles Shields
examples: A 2018 Charles Shields book notably called this author 'The man who wrote the perfect novel'
a 1965 novel by this author about a English professor

works.name: Butcher's Crossing
works.description: A frontier novel in which the protagonist is reminded of a prostitute after seeing a skinned buffalo. McDonald tells the protagonist 'You get born, and you nurse on lies, and you wean on lies' after he returns to the title locale.

clues.clue: Protagonist reminded of a prostitute after seeing a skinned buffalo
examples: the protagonist is reminded of a prostitute after seeing a skinned buffalo

clues.clue: McDonald: 'You get born, and you nurse on lies, and you wean on lies'
examples: McDonald tells the protagonist, 'You get born, and you nurse on lies, and you wean on lies' after he returns to the title locale

works.name: Augustus
works.description: An epistolary novel about the Roman emperor Augustus (Octavian). Only the last few letters are written by Octavian himself.

clues.clue: Epistolary novel about Augustus; only the last few letters are by Octavian

works.name: Nothing but the Night
works.description: Williams's first novel, which he later disowned. A character in it described being young as a time when 'living is a simple, perfect succession of golden days.'

clues.clue: Novel disowned by the author; a character describes youth as 'a simple, perfect succession of golden days'
examples: One character in a novel that was disowned by this author described being young as a time when 'living is a simple, perfect succession of golden days'

comprehensive_summary:
John Williams was an American novelist and academic best known for Stoner (1965), a novel about William Stoner, a University of Missouri English professor. The clues identify it as one of the great rediscovered novels of American literature: a 2018 Charles Shields biography called Williams 'The man who wrote the perfect novel,' and the book saw a dramatic rise in popularity after its 2013 reissue by NYRB Classics. Anna Gavalda's French translation helped make it a surprise bestseller in Europe.

In Stoner, the protagonist is inspired to study literature by Shakespeare's Sonnet 73. He has an affair with Kathrine Driscoll and feuds with his crippled colleague Hollis Lomax (Willy Lomax) for twenty years after aggressively interrogating a student during Lomax's thesis defense.

Williams also wrote Butcher's Crossing, a frontier novel in which the protagonist returns to the title locale and hears McDonald say 'You get born, and you nurse on lies, and you wean on lies.' He sees a skinned buffalo that reminds him of a prostitute. His epistolary novel Augustus reconstructs the life of the Roman emperor through letters, with only the final few written by Octavian himself. His first novel, Nothing but the Night, was later disowned by the author.

Note: Many clue sets that mention 'John Williams' also reference the famous American film composer (Star Wars, Superman, Jaws) and the Australian classical guitarist, who are entirely different people.

cards.front: Novel: the protagonist, a University of Missouri English professor, is inspired to study literature by Shakespeare's Sonnet 73
Novel: the protagonist feuds with the crippled colleague Hollis Lomax for 20 years after savagely interrogating a student during a thesis defense
Novel: the protagonist has an affair with Kathrine Driscoll; a 2018 biography called the author 'The man who wrote the perfect novel'
Novel: saw a dramatic rise in popularity after its 2013 reissue by NYRB Classics; a French translation by Anna Gavalda made it a bestseller in Europe
Novel: McDonald tells the protagonist 'You get born, and you nurse on lies, and you wean on lies' after he returns to the title frontier locale
Novel: an epistolary novel about the Roman emperor in which only the last few letters are written by Octavian himself
